actionable_feedback: Add an explicit numeric rounding and precision policy in the brief and CSV metadata. Specify how many decimal places apply to intermediate normalized terms and final score, and round consistently (e.g., round intermediate terms to 4 decimals, final scores to 2 decimals). In the artifacts, include a short table showing classification invariance under that rounding; if any row flips, mark it needs_review.

Resolve or more fully document the metric-mapping ambiguity: either cite the canonical mapping from the embedded packet/stakeholder, or include a concise decision-tree that lists the alternative plausible mappings (e.g., treat payapp_pct as lidar confirmation gap vs. another interpretation), recompute scores under each, and show the impact on classification. Keep needs_review where ambiguity remains but make the alternatives explicit.
Ensure the superintendent_brief.md contains the full, untruncated row table (complete R4 row and any trailing explanation). Confirm the delivered file size and preview include the complete table.

Clarify the operational interpretation for option O3 (overbill margin improvement): state whether it is applied multiplicatively to normalized margin, whether the improvement is capped (e.g., cannot exceed 100% normalized), and show the arithmetic used in post-mitigation recomputations in the CSV.

Provide a separate machine-readable sensitivity_test_summary CSV (columns: record_id, base_score, rounded_base_score, favorable_shift_score, adverse_shift_score, classification_changes_flag) so automated verifiers can ingest sensitivity results without relying on the brief text.

Add a short validation section in the brief describing verification checks run (e.g., unit-test script name or snippet) that re-computes CSV scores from raw inputs and confirms equality to committed CSV values; attach or reference the deterministic_precheck result.
Fix any minor textual issues and ensure all numerical formatting is consistent across the brief and CSV (commas in thousands, consistent decimal separators).
artifact_specific_comments: progress_deviation.csv: Contains required intermediate columns and sensitivity outputs; include an additional column documenting rounded intermediate terms (using the declared rounding policy) and a boolean 'rounding_sensitive' flag if any classification flips under rounding.

superintendent_brief.md: Well-structured executive brief but the preview is truncated. Confirm that the submitted file includes the full review-ready row table (complete R4 row) and the deterministic normalization section includes the rounding policy and the decision-tree or mapping citation.
feedback_summary: Baseline attempt produced the required artifacts and applied a clear deterministic normalization and scoring formula; per-row traceability, sensitivity testing, remediation cost arithmetic, and expert-review flags are present. Minor completeness issues reduce semantic polish: (1) no explicit numeric rounding/precision policy or demonstration that rounding will not flip classifications; (2) unresolved ambiguity mapping four supplied raw fields to three scoring drivers (correctly flagged as needs_review but requires a short decision-tree or authoritative citation); (3) the superintendent brief preview is truncated (R4 row cut) so ensure the delivered brief is complete; (4) clarify interpretation and cap for O3 margin improvement and provide a separate machine-readable sensitivity summary CSV for automated verifiers.

extra_model_fields.notes: Baseline meets core rubric requirements; recommended local fixes aim to remove minor semantic uncertainty and improve automated verifiability.
